---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: ca60f126b5c0720f22f6274d7a47da88125c6b91
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-4 justifies the live-lock skip as this-run-only ('a single worktree that happens to be mid-use exactly when this particular invocation ran'), but story dc-2 holds the lock for the managed worktree's WHOLE LIFETIME of the owning serve process, not for the duration of use. Under a long-running serve, a merged or deleted worktree therefore stays skipped for the entire serve uptime — reclamation on parent dc-4's ratified signals is silently narrowed to reclamation-after-owner-exit, an unbounded deferral that dc-4's 'temporary, this-run-only' framing understates and no exempts edge declares against spec/workbench-directory dc-4. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 makes managed worktrees reclaim-eligible the instant the merged/deleted signal fires, reading verdi-store-layout's retention_days bullet as reserved exclusively for the derived-cache prune. That scoping claim about another spec's ratified gc section is asserted, not edged: if verdi-store-layout's Garbage collection text applies derived.retention_days (or any grace period) to merged/deleted refs generally rather than to data/derived/\u003cref\u003e/ alone, instant worktree reclamation contradicts it with no declared supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/verdi-store-layout }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5, spec/worktree-manager#dc-1, spec/worktree-manager#dc-2, spec/worktree-manager#dc-3, spec/worktree-manager#dc-4, spec/worktree-manager#dc-5] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"cf-1\",\"text\":\"Story dc-4 justifies the live-lock skip as this-run-only ('a single worktree that happens to be mid-use exactly when this particular invocation ran'), but story dc-2 holds the lock for the managed worktree's WHOLE LIFETIME of the owning serve process, not for the duration of use. Under a long-running serve, a merged or deleted worktree therefore stays skipped for the entire serve uptime — reclamation on parent dc-4's ratified signals is silently narrowed to reclamation-after-owner-exit, an unbounded deferral that dc-4's 'temporary, this-run-only' framing understates and no exempts edge declares against spec/workbench-directory dc-4.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-3 makes managed worktrees reclaim-eligible the instant the merged/deleted signal fires, reading verdi-store-layout's retention_days bullet as reserved exclusively for the derived-cache prune. That scoping claim about another spec's ratified gc section is asserted, not edged: if verdi-store-layout's Garbage collection text applies derived.retention_days (or any grace period) to merged/deleted refs generally rather than to data/derived/\u003cref\u003e/ alone, instant worktree reclamation contradicts it with no declared supersedes/exempts edge.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/verdi-store-layout\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-4 justifies the live-lock skip as this-run-only ('a single worktree that happens to be mid-use exactly when this particular invocation ran'), but story dc-2 holds the lock for the managed worktree's WHOLE LIFETIME of the owning serve process, not for the duration of use. Under a long-running serve, a merged or deleted worktree therefore stays skipped for the entire serve uptime — reclamation on parent dc-4's ratified signals is silently narrowed to reclamation-after-owner-exit, an unbounded deferral that dc-4's 'temporary, this-run-only' framing understates and no exempts edge declares against spec/workbench-directory dc-4. (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 makes managed worktrees reclaim-eligible the instant the merged/deleted signal fires, reading verdi-store-layout's retention_days bullet as reserved exclusively for the derived-cache prune. That scoping claim about another spec's ratified gc section is asserted, not edged: if verdi-store-layout's Garbage collection text applies derived.retention_days (or any grace period) to merged/deleted refs generally rather than to data/derived/<ref>/ alone, instant worktree reclamation contradicts it with no declared supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.30)
